name: Capsicum
summary: Pepper species used for topical and metabolic effects.
description: Pepper species used for topical and metabolic effects.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.
contraindications: Broken or inflamed skin for topical use
interactions: Additive skin irritation with other topical rubs
oral products may theoretically compound antihypertensive or anticoagulant regimens

sources.title: Peppers and their constituents against obesity
Food-grade red chili extract in overweight subjects
sources.citation: Capsifen 200 mg/day providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days; n=24

sources.title: Estimation of Dietary Capsaicinoid Exposure in Korea and Assessment of Its Health Effects
sources.citation: Mean capsaicinoid intake 3.25 mg/day; abdominal pain reported; 30 mg/day may be max tolerable for long-term repeated consumption

claimMap.claim: A sustained-release red chili extract formulation providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days was studied for appetite/body-weight outcomes in overweight adults.
claimMap.notes: A sustained-release red chili extract formulation providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days was studied for appetite/body-weight outcomes in overweight adults.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Capsifen 200 mg once daily providing 4 mg capsaicinoids/day for 28 days

claimMap.claim: Capsaicinoid dosing is dose-dependent and context-specific; typical dietary exposure may be below adverse-effect levels, but long-term higher-dose supplementation needs caution.
claimMap.notes: Capsaicinoid dosing is dose-dependent and context-specific; typical dietary exposure may be below adverse-effect levels, but long-term higher-dose supplementation needs caution.

qualifiers.dose_or_duration: Estimated typical intake 1–30 mg capsaicinoids/day; clinical studies used 2–135 mg; 30 mg may be maximum tolerable dose for repeated long-term consumption in this context
qualifiers.population: Korean population dietary intake estimate; clinical studies summarized
